badge: A practice ground. Not a course.
subtitle: Speexify is a practice ground for adults who want to actually use a language. Not study one more time. Live sessions with a coach. Real conversations, not drills. Just the reps you've been missing.
pricing_title: Choose the practice rhythm you can keep.
pricing_subtitle: Most members start with private coaching, then build a routine around one or two live sessions a week. No hidden fees, no vague consultation wall before you can see the numbers.
pricing_from_hint: Best value on the Intensive package
pricing_region_note: Showing {currency} pricing for {region}. Full regional pricing is available on the packages page.
pricing_team_text: Build a cohort plan with needs analysis, reporting, mixed 1:1 and group practice, and a program manager your team actually knows.
features_title: It's not a course. It's a practice ground.
features_subtitle: You've already tried the apps. You've already sat through the classrooms. They taught you the language. Speexify is where you finally use it.
feature1_text: Every session is a real conversation with a coach who knows your goals, your work, your voice. No drills. No scripts. No 'repeat after me.' Just the reps you actually need.
bento_stat_text: Members reach what they came for 2.7× faster than people self-studying. Most feel the shift inside four sessions.
feature2_title: We measure the meeting, not the streak.
feature2_text: No vanity streaks. No daily-goal guilt. We track the meeting you led. The interview you landed. The call you stopped dreading.
feature3_text: Sessions when you're ready, not when a course tells you to be ready. Reschedule. Pause. Come back. Your spot stays.
feature4_title: Built around you, not a syllabus.
feature4_text: Prepping for an interview next week? Leading your first meeting in English next month? Your practice plan starts from your real life, not a textbook.
how_subtitle: From your first session to sounding like yourself in another language.
how_step1_label: Tell us what you need to say
how_step1_title: Tell us what you need to say.
how_step1_text: A short call. We listen for what you actually need: at work, in interviews, in life. Then we shape your practice plan around it.
how_step2_text: We pair you with a coach who fits your goals, your industry, your voice. Not random. Chosen.
how_step3_text: Live sessions, one or two a week. Real conversations, every time. Within a month you'll hear the change. Soon after, so will everyone else.
curr_subtitle: Not grammar drills. Not vocabulary lists. The conversations you've been avoiding, turned into reps.
curr1_desc: The conversations that fill a day at work. Less translating in your head, more thinking in the language.
curr2_desc: The kind of room where what you say is remembered. Practice the framing, the pauses, the close. Out loud, in front of a coach.
curr3_desc: Emails that get replies. Messages that match your seniority. Writing that sounds like the person you actually are.
curr4_desc: Job interviews. Visa interviews. The conversation that decides something. Rehearsed out loud until it stops feeling like one.
coaches_subtitle: Every coach here has been in the room you're afraid of, and run it. They've sat through the interview. Led the meeting. Closed the deal. In a language that wasn't their first.
coaches_note: Your coach isn't a tutor. They're a coach.
coach1_bio: A decade coaching teams inside corporations. Knows the meeting you're avoiding.
coach2_bio: Former consultant. Helps you say less and mean more. Specialises in pitches, decks, and the room.
coach3_bio: Led international teams in two languages. Coaches the version of you that runs the room.
shift_subtitle: Watch what happens when you stop studying and start speaking.
shift1_before: Sorry, can someone else take this? My English isn't...
shift1_after: I'll kick us off. Here's where we are and what we need.
shift1_desc: The engineer who used to mute himself now runs the client calls.
shift2_before: Umm... so what I mean is... maybe we could... sorry.
shift2_after: Here's what I think. We should ship this and test with users.
shift2_desc: The team lead who hesitated now says what she means, first time.
shift3_before: I don't think anyone notices what I do in English anyway.
shift3_after: My manager pulled me aside and asked what changed.
shift3_desc: The quiet contributor whose manager started asking what changed.
testi1_quote: I used to freeze in meetings. Now I'm usually the one who starts the conversation. The English was always there. Speexify just gave me somewhere to actually use it.
testi2_quote: Two months here did more for me than ten years of studying. The difference is simple: I was actually speaking, every session.
testi3_quote: I came in expecting grammar drills. I got someone who listened to how I actually sound, and worked on that. Different game.
testi4_quote: I stopped translating in my head about six weeks in. Now I just say what I mean. That alone changed everything at work.
faq_subtitle: The questions people ask before their first session.
faq1_a: We look at what you need to say, where, and to whom. Then we pick the coach whose background and style fits. Nothing random.
faq2_a: Anytime. No conversation required. Your notes and progress move with you.
faq3_a: Then this is the right place. The whole point is to start using what you already have. Your coach meets you where you are, not where a textbook says you should be.
faq4_a: One or two sessions a week, 30 to 60 minutes each. Enough to feel the shift inside a month.
newsletter_title: A short letter, once a week.
newsletter_sub: Quiet observations on speaking, listening, and being understood. One paragraph. No noise.
cta_sub: Book your first session. Try the practice ground. The first one is on us.
demo_body: No watching. No passive listening. Every session is live, one coach at a time, focused on what you actually need to say next week.
demo_feature_1: Live video sessions, one coach at a time
demo_feature_5: A progress view focused on what you actually use
compare_subtitle: You've probably tried at least one of these. Here's what each one is actually for.
testimonials_subtitle: Read enough of these and you'll recognise yourself in at least one.
